body: PT Garuda Maintenance Facility Aero Asia Tbk reported a 556.19% net profit growth in the first semester of 2024, reaching $13.30 million, driven by a significant increase in revenue to $216.47 million, up from $166.90 million in the same period last year. The company's profit before tax also rose to $13.28 million, with revenue contributors from repair and overhaul, maintenance, and other operations all showing significant increases.
title: Garuda Maintenance Facility Aero Asia Records 556% Net Profit Growth in First Semester 2024.

body: PT Lautan Luas Tbk will repay its remaining IDR 315 billion shelf registration bonds, maturing on November 12, 2024, using a combination of internal and external funds, which will positively impact the company's financial condition in the future. As of June 30, 2024, the company had IDR 227.6 billion in cash and cash equivalents, and had posted IDR 3.71 trillion in revenue and IDR 136.56 billion in net profit for the first semester of 2024.
title: PT Lautan Luas Tbk to Repay IDR 315 Billion Bonds in 2024 Using Internal and External Funds

body: PT Net Visi Media Tbk is undergoing a 2:1 reverse stock merger, reducing the number of shares from 23.45 billion to 11.72 billion, with the new nominal value per share increasing from IDR 100 to IDR 200. The merger, approved by shareholders, aims to improve performance, following the recent acquisition of 80.05% of the company's shares by PT MD Entertainment Tbk, a production house owned by the Punjabi family.
title: PT Net Visi Media to Conduct 2:1 Reverse Stock Merger, Reducing Shares by 11.72 Billion

body: PT Matahari Putra Prima Tbk (MPPA) has injected IDR 7.5 billion in additional capital into its subsidiary, PT Matahari Super Ekonomi (MSE), increasing MPPA's total issued and paid-up capital in MSE to IDR 9.98 billion. As of the first semester of 2024, MPPA had total assets of IDR 3.45 trillion, cash and cash equivalents of IDR 198.48 billion, and total equity of IDR 201.78 billion.
title: Matahari Putra Prima Injects IDR 7.5 Billion into Subsidiary Matahari Super Ekonomi

body: Foreign investors maintained a net buying position on Wednesday, October 16, with a total net volume of 70.86 million shares, driven by purchases of 129.19 million shares of PT MNC Land Tbk and 148.23 million shares of PT Telkom Indonesia Tbk. Meanwhile, foreign selling was led by PT Goto Gojek Tokopedia Tbk, with a net volume of -597.40 million shares, followed by PT Bumi Resources Minerals Tbk and PT Alam Sutera Realty Tbk.
title: Foreign Net Volume Remains Consistent in Green Zone with Mixed Buying and Selling Activities

body: Indonesian Securities Rating Agency, Pefindo, has confirmed a "selective default" rating for PT Waskita Karya TBK, citing the company's very weak financial profile, with a debt-to-equity ratio of 11.5x as of June 30, 2024. The rating may be revised if the company completes its restructuring program or demonstrates its ability to meet financial obligations in a sustainable manner.
title: Indonesian Securities Rating Agency Downgrades Waskita Karya's Credit Rating Due to Weak Financial Profile

body: PT OKI Pulp & Paper Mills, a subsidiary of Sinarmas Group, plans to issue IDR 2.25 trillion shelf-registration bond and USD 7.95 million shelf-registration bond, with coupons ranging from 7.50% to 10.75% per annum for IDR bond and 5.75% to 8% per annum for USD bond, with tenors varying from 370 days to 5 years. The bonds have received "ida+" and "idaa-" ratings from Pemeringkat Efek Indonesia and Kredit Rating Indonesia, respectively, and will be offered to the public on October 24-25, 2024, with listing on October 31, 2024.
title: PT OKI Pulp & Paper Mills to Issue IDR 2.25 Trillion and USD 7.95 Million Bonds in October 2024.

body: Foreign investors purchased a net 41.01 million shares, with top buys including PT Bumi Resources Minerals TBK (107.99 million shares), PT Kawasan Industri Jababeka TBK (81.60 million shares), and PT MNC Land TBK (68.26 million shares). On the selling side, PT Goto Gojek Tokopedia TBK saw the most shares sold (648.12 million shares, net volume -338.57 million shares), followed by PT Ciputra Development TBK and PT Ingria Pratama Capitalindo TBK.
title: Foreign investors buy 41.01 million shares in Indonesian market, led by purchases in mining and industrial sectors.
